skill.name: commit
skill.description: Generate concise Git commit messages from staged changes, following the project style and conventional commit types.
skill.summary: Create project-aware Git commit messages from staged changes.
skill.author: DavidOpDeBeeck

security_audit.summary: Static analysis correctly identified the real Git inspection and commit workflow, but many backtick hits are Markdown examples rather than executable code. The weak-cryptography alerts are false positives, and no malicious intent, network exfiltration, environment access, or prompt injection was found.

medium_findings.title: External Git Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to run Git commands to inspect staged changes, read repository status, read recent history, and execute a commit after user approval. This is legitimate for a commit-message skill, but it can expose staged code and modify repository history.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The listed lines explicitly instruct use of Git commands and a commit action. The commands are repository-scoped and user-facing, with no evidence of shell interpolation or network exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick External Command False Positives
low_findings.description: Several external-command alerts are Markdown code fences, commit type labels, and commit message examples. They document message formatting and naming patterns; they do not instruct shell execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are fenced examples or inline formatting guidance in Markdown. They contain no executable command invocation, shell expansion, or user-input interpolation.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Pattern False Positive
low_findings.description: The static weak-cryptography alerts do not correspond to any cryptographic algorithm or hashing operation in the skill. The cited lines are frontmatter text, commit-message placeholders, writing guidance, and example wording.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of the cited lines found no MD5, SHA-1, cipher use, password hashing, or security-sensitive cryptographic code. These alerts appear to be keyword matches in prose.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Git Command Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs the assistant to run Git commands to inspect staged changes, read repository status, read recent history, and execute a commit after user approval. This is legitimate for a commit-message skill, but it can expose staged code and modify repository history.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The listed lines explicitly instruct use of Git commands and a commit action. The commands are repository-scoped and user-facing, with no evidence of shell interpolation or network exfiltration.

content.value_statement: Writing clear commit messages from staged changes takes time and consistency. This skill reviews Git context and drafts concise messages that match project conventions.
actual_capabilities: Reviews staged Git changes before drafting a commit message.
Checks repository status to understand unstaged and staged work.
Uses recent commit history to match local message style.
Drafts conventional commit types such as feat, fix, test, and docs.
Asks for approval before running the commit action.
Suggests splitting unrelated changes into separate commits.
limitations: Requires a Git repository with staged changes.
Does not validate build, tests, or runtime behavior.
May miss business intent that is not visible in the diff.
Can create a commit only when the user approves it.

use_cases.title: Prepare Clean Commits
use_cases.description: Draft a concise message after staging a focused code change.

use_cases.title: Match Team Conventions
use_cases.description: Use recent history to keep commit messages consistent across a repository.

use_cases.title: Review Mixed Changes
use_cases.description: Identify unrelated staged changes and decide whether to split them before committing.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Commit Message
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my staged changes and suggest a concise commit message.

prompt_templates.title: Use Project Style
prompt_templates.prompt: Review staged changes and recent commits, then suggest a message that matches this repository style.

prompt_templates.title: Check Commit Scope
prompt_templates.scenario: Validate whether staged work belongs together.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my staged changes and tell me whether they should be one commit or split into multiple commits.

prompt_templates.title: Commit After Approval
prompt_templates.prompt: Review staged changes, propose the best commit message, ask for my approval, then commit if I approve.

output_examples.input: Staged changes add a login validation test and update related fixtures.
Staged changes include a new export button and matching UI state updates.
output_examples.output: feat: add export button state handling

output_examples.input: Staged changes contain unrelated backend fixes and documentation updates.
output_examples.output: The skill recommends splitting the staged work into separate fix and docs commits.

best_practices: Stage only one logical change before invoking the skill.
Review the proposed message before approving any commit action.
Run tests separately when the change affects behavior.
anti_patterns: Do not use it to commit unrelated changes together.
Do not approve a commit before reviewing staged files yourself.
Do not treat the message as proof that the code is correct.

faq.question: Does this skill create commits automatically?
faq.answer: It should ask for approval before executing the commit action.

faq.question: Does it work without staged changes?
faq.answer: No. It is designed to review staged changes before drafting a message.

faq.question: Can it follow conventional commits?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses types such as feat, fix, refactor, test, docs, style, and chore.

faq.question: Does it run tests before committing?
faq.answer: No. Testing is outside the described capability and should be run separately.

faq.question: Why is the risk level medium?
faq.answer: The skill runs Git commands and can modify repository history after approval.

faq.question: Does it send code to external services?
faq.answer: No evidence of network requests or external data transfer was found in SKILL.md.
